pattern: Counting decodings is a one-dimensional dp where each position adds the ways from taking one digit and, when valid, the ways from taking a two-digit pair.
hints: Letters map from 1 to 26, so a single digit 1-9 is one letter and a two-digit chunk 10-26 is another.
The number of ways to decode up to position i depends on whether the last one or two digits form a valid letter.
Let dp[i] be ways for the first i characters: add dp[i-1] when s[i-1] is 1-9, and add dp[i-2] when s[i-2:i] is between 10 and 26; a leading or stranded zero contributes nothing.

edge_inputs.description: two ways for a simple two-digit string
three ways with an internal split choice
a leading zero cannot be decoded at all
zero must pair with the preceding digit or fail
valid because the zero joins as twenty
a 27 pair is invalid so only one decoding exists

stress.description: long digit string biased to produce many valid splits without leading-zero traps
stress.note: first digit forced non-zero so the whole string is decodable; interior zeros still stress pairing
